title: Testcase accreditation & limited-use transfer — MAP and timeline
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Kevin Li <kevin.li@nova-analytics.com>, Maria Gonzales <maria.gonzales@nova-analytics.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 09:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Testcase accreditation & limited-use transfer — MAP and timeline

Kevin / Maria — great talking yesterday. Per our discussion, attached is a proposed MAP (nova_map_v1.xlsx) that maps the testcase subsets we can share, acceptance gates, and the close timeline for the evaluation exchange. High level:

- Redwood provides an ephemeral subset of our testcases (testcase_subset_202605.tar.gz) for Nova to run a single closed-run benchmark in your staging environment.
- Transfer is gated on: an executed MPA clause (see sheet 'Gates'), acknowledgement of sandbox retention rules, and acceptance of the usage constraints PDF (redwood_usage-constraints-202605.pdf).
- Target close: dataset transfer complete and benchmark started by 2026-05-25.

Please review the MAP and the usage constraints. Two asks: (1) confirm the acceptance gate owners on your side, (2) share preferred S3 user principal for transfer (we'll initiate a pre-signed S3 copy with time-limited access).

Attachments: nova_map_v1.xlsx (MAP), testcase_subset_202605.tar.gz (sample pack), redwood_usage-constraints-202605.pdf (legal/ops constraints)

Thanks — Ava
Ava Chen
Enterprise Solutions – Redwood Inference

From: Kevin Li <kevin.li@nova-analytics.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Maria Gonzales <maria.gonzales@nova-analytics.com>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 11:03:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Testcase accreditation & limited-use transfer — MAP and timeline

Ava — thanks, the MAP looks sensible. Quick questions before we loop legal/ops:  

1) The MAP notes a 30-day retention in our staging environment — can you confirm that restriction applies to derivative models too?
2) For the benchmark run, can we provision a single-run license that allows us to log outputs for internal analysis (no downstream distribution)?

If both items are clear, we can route the MAP into our procurement + security queue and aim for the 05/25 kickoff. S3 principal: integrations@nova-analytics.com (we can provide an IAM principal ARN if needed).

Kevin

On a separate note: can you confirm whether Redwood wants CNF redlines versus a short form MPA?

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Kevin Li <kevin.li@nova-analytics.com>
Cc: Rohan Mehta <rohan_mehta@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 14:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Testcase accreditation & limited-use transfer — MAP and timeline

Thanks Kevin — good questions. Looping in Rohan (ops) and Monica (legal) to confirm exact language. Quick answers from product/sales perspective: 

- Retention: our standard MAP for ephemeral evals is 30 days for both raw testcases and any model outputs produced during the benchmark. Derivatives (fine-tuned checkpoints) are explicitly prohibited under the usage constraints — the PDF contains the clause: 'No checkpointing, fine-tuning, or external redistribution of outputs.'
- Single-run logging: allowed for internal analysis only; logs must be stored in your sandbox that meets our ephemeral retention covenant (30 days) and cannot be shared externally.

We'll accept either: (A) a short-form MPA that references Redwood's usage constraints PDF and your internal PO, or (B) CNF redlines if procurement requires it. Monica / Rohan — can you confirm which approach is faster from legal/ops? Also Kevin — please send the IAM principal ARN when ready so we can pre-configure the transfer bucket policy.

Ava

From: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Kevin Li <kevin.li@nova-analytics.com>
Cc: Rohan Mehta <rohan_mehta@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 19 May 2026 10:08:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Testcase accreditation & limited-use transfer — MAP and timeline

Kevin / Ava — quick legal read and redlines attached (see summary below). I kept this intentionally short to avoid delaying the transfer: 

- Short-form MPA is adequate provided Nova signs a one-paragraph acceptance that references Redwood's usage constraints PDF (we'll add signature block to the MAP).
- Add explicit clause: 'No derivative training or checkpoint storage; any model outputs stored for analysis must be deleted at day 30.'
- For auditability, we request a single post-benchmark attestation from Nova confirming deletion and a CSV manifest of buckets accessed.

If you want the CNF strikes, we can do that, but it will add 2–3 business days. I recommend short-form to hit the 05/25 target.

Attachments: redwood_usage-constraints-202605.pdf (same as provided), MAP_signable_v1.xlsx (MAP with signature block)

—Monica
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference

From: Kevin Li <kevin.li@nova-analytics.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Maria Gonzales <maria.gonzales@nova-analytics.com>, Rohan Mehta <rohan_mehta@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 20 May 2026 09:16:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Testcase accreditation & limited-use transfer — MAP and timeline

Monica — thanks, that approach works for us. We can sign the short-form MPA and provide the post-benchmark attestation. Confirming details:

- IAM principal ARN: arn:aws:iam::987654321012:role/nova-integration-sandbox
- We accept the 30-day deletion window and the prohibition on checkpointing.
- We'll schedule one benchmark run the morning of 2026-05-25 (start window 09:00–12:00 ET).

We'll sign MAP_signable_v1.xlsx and return the DocuSign link by EOD today. Please advise a target time when Redwood will enable the pre-signed copy.

Thanks — Kevin

From: Rohan Mehta <rohan_mehta@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Kevin Li <kevin.li@nova-analytics.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 21 May 2026 14:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Testcase accreditation & limited-use transfer — MAP and timeline

Kevin — thanks, ARN received and all set. We'll: 

1) Stand up the pre-signed S3 transfer by 2026-05-24 18:00 PT with a 48-hour access window.
2) Seed the bucket with testcase_subset_202605.tar.gz and a transfer manifest.
3) Watch for the signed MAP (DocuSign); we'll only enable the bucket when the signature completes.
4) Post-benchmark, we'll provide a short attestation template you can fill and return.

Closing note: target timeline remains benchmark start 2026-05-25; internal ops will monitor and disable access as soon as attestation is received or after 30 days per the MAP.

—Rohan
Infrastructure Ops, Redwood Inference

title: MAP: Cost preflight — tokens/$ baseline and cache/batch gates
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samantha Reed <samantha@acuityhealth.com>, Acuity Procurement <procurement@acuityhealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 15 May 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: MAP: Cost preflight — tokens/$ baseline and cache/batch gates

Hi Samantha / team,

Thanks again for the time earlier today. Attached is a first-pass workbook (acuity_map_cost_workbook_v1.xlsx) linked to the MAP we discussed. High level summary:

- Baseline token mix: 60% short-form prompts (~50 tokens output), 30% multi-turn chat (~220 tokens total), 10% embeddings/rerank.
- Current tokens per $ estimate: 6.8k tokens per $ (hosted model profile) — this uses our standard quantization and default batching.
- Prefetch/KV cache assumptions captured on sheet 'cache_assumptions' — optimistic hit-rate: 35%, conservative: 18%.
- Batching: we modeled two operating points: low-latency (batch size <= 4, throughput tradeoff) and cost-first (batch size ~16, 2–3x tokens/$ improvement in steady-state).

As part of the MAP acceptance we recommend the following gating checks (so they can be tracked in the mutual action plan):
1) Unit econ sanity: run the provided 'tokens-per-$' sim with your traffic sampler (sheet 'traffic_sampler') and validate within ±15% of our baseline.
2) Cache verification: verify a 48-hour prefix/KV hit-rate sample from a pilot environment. Goal: >=20% for initial acceptance.
3) Latency SLO impact: two SLOs — p95 < 220ms (low-latency path) and p95 < 700ms (cost path).

Next steps I propose:
- You: confirm which acceptance SLO matters most (latency or cost).
- We: run a short 48-hour prefeth/batch sim against a sample payload you provide and share a v2 workbook with scenario outputs.

I've looped Marissa for any commercial notes you want us to include (discount cadence / pilot credits).

Attachments: acuity_map_cost_workbook_v1.xlsx (workbook, 3 sheets)

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales — Redwood Inference
ben@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555‑0112

--
Redwood Inference | developer-first inference runtime


From: Samantha Reed <samantha@acuityhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Acuity Procurement <procurement@acuityhealth.com>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 10:04:00 -0400
Subject: Re: MAP: Cost preflight — tokens/$ baseline and cache/batch gates

Ben — thanks for the workbook and the clear next steps. Quick clarifying questions before we run our sampler:
1) The 6.8k tokens/$ benchmark — is that measured with your hosted small-quant model (redwood-small-8bit) or the medium variant? Our model selection will affect the tokens/$ numbers.
2) On batching: our UX team is sensitive to tail latency. At what batch size do you start to see p95 degrade above 300ms for the typical 220-token chat case?
3) For the cache verification, do you need historical traffic or can we send a sampled replay (anonymized) to your SEs?

Samantha Reed
Head of AI Integrations, Acuity Health
samantha@acuityhealth.com


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samantha Reed <samantha@acuityhealth.com>
Cc: Acuity Procurement <procurement@acuityhealth.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 19 May 2026 08:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP: Cost preflight — tokens/$ baseline and cache/batch gates

Samantha — good questions, quick answers below and I've attached v2 of the workbook with two model profiles and a small annotated sim (acuity_map_cost_workbook_v2.xlsx).

1) 6.8k tokens/$ is using redwood-small-8bit with our default kernel selections. The medium profile (redwood-medium-4bit-quant) sits closer to ~3.1k tokens/$. I've added both numbers on the 'model_profiles' tab.
2) Tail latency: in our measured internal sims for 220-token chat, p95 stays under 300ms up to batch size 6 on the low-latency routing path; beyond batch size 8 p95 begins to rise toward 450–600ms depending on concurrency. See 'batch_latency_tradeoffs' tab for the plotted curves.
3) A sampled replay (anonymized) is perfect and preferred — it lets us run the traffic sampler and provide a simulation you can validate. If you can share ~50k requests (anonymized), we can turn around the sim in 48 hours.

Proposed MAP updates (I added them in the 'MAP_tracker' sheet of v2):
- Acceptance gate A: tokens-per-$ within ±15% on the chosen model profile (owner: Acuity / validation by Redwood SE).
- Acceptance gate B: observed KV/prefix hit-rate >= 20% over 48-hour replay.
- Acceptance gate C: Latency SLO p95 as agreed (we default to p95 < 300ms for low-latency path).

If that all looks good I'll schedule a 30-minute sandbox handoff with Monica (SE) so she can collect the replay and run the sim.

Thanks,
Ben

On Mon, May 18, 2026 at 10:04 AM Samantha Reed <samantha@acuityhealth.com> wrote:
> Ben — thanks for the workbook and the clear next steps. Quick clarifying questions before we run our sampler:
> ...



From: Monica Patel <monica@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samantha Reed <samantha@acuityhealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 20 May 2026 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP: Cost preflight — tokens/$ baseline and cache/batch gates

Hi Samantha / team — Monica from SE here, thanks for looping me in. Short technical note ahead of the replay handoff so the MAP gates are clear operationally.

Summary of the preflight sim plan (what we'll deliver):
- Run the anonymized replay through our sampler tooling (48 hours wall clock equivalent) and produce: tokens-per-$ distribution, per-endpoint cache-hit heatmap, and batch-size vs latency curves.
- Seed a controlled prefix cache during the replay run to estimate warmup behavior (this is how we separate cache-improvement from cold-start penalties).

Key technical assumptions captured up-front (these will be inputs into the MAP acceptance checks):
- Prefetch/KV cache behavior: 3 tiers (cold, warm, hot) — we expect most prefixes to fall in warm after ~12 hours under steady traffic. We model warm hit-rate at 28% in the workbook's default scenario.
- Batching concurrency model: we simulate client-side concurrency at your observed 0.9 RPS per user average; throughput gains appear beyond batch 8 only when concurrency > 25%.
- Safety nets: if tokens-per-$ deviates more than 25% vs. expectation, we will switch routing to the dedicated cost path (higher batch, lower cost) for the pilot to preserve economics while we triage accuracy regressions.

Acceptance deliverables we'll attach to the MAP record after the run:
- se-prefetch-batching-sim-summary.pdf (short PDF with key charts and gating verdicts)
- acuity_map_cost_workbook_v2.xlsx (scenario outputs and annotated assumptions)

Logistics: please upload the anonymized replay to the Drive folder linked in the MAP (https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1Acuity-MAP-2026) and ping me when done. I can run the sim within 48 hours of receiving the replay and will share the sim-summary and a suggested gating verdict (pass / conditional / fail) for each acceptance gate.

Thanks — looking forward to the handoff.
Monica Patel
Solutions Engineer — Redwood Inference
monica@redwoodinference.com | +1 (650) 555‑0199

--
se-prefetch-batching-sim-summary.pdf (attachment stub)

title: Velori — request for trial top-up + credits (exec approval)
preview:
From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Tom Richards <tom.richards@velori.ai>, Sara Liu <sara@velori.ai>
Date: Tue, 19 May 2026 09:03:00 -0700
Subject: Velori — request for trial top-up + credits (exec approval)

Hi Tom / Sara,

Thanks again for taking the demo last week — loved seeing the billing-reconciliation use case you built on top of Redwood. You mentioned the integration test run next week is the gating factor for a short pilot decision. To make that smoother, I wanted to propose a limited onramp sweetener so you can run the end-to-end tests and some traffic without hitting hard limits.

Proposal (what I'm authorized to offer today):
- 100,000 free token credits applied to your account (usable across models)
- 30-day trial extension from your current expiry date
- A dedicated onboarding session with our solutions engineer (2 hours)

If that isn't sufficient to complete the integration testing, I can escalate for executive approval for an expanded package. To move quickly: can you send over a short list of the integration tests you need and an estimated token burn? Also attached is a quick checklist we use for onramp success.

If you're available, I can put 15 minutes on the calendar to align next steps.

Best,
Alex Martinez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
alex@redwood.com | (415) 555-0124
Redwood Inference

Attachment: velori_onramp_checklist.pdf (attachment stub)


From: Tom Richards <tom.richards@velori.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 19 May 2026 11:10:42 -0700
Subject: Re: Velori — request for trial top-up + credits (exec approval)

Alex — thanks, appreciate the quick reply and the 100k credit offer. A couple things we ran into while re-playing the tests:

- Our integration stress runs (including synthetic traffic and data transforms) will likely burn ~220k tokens over the next 3 weeks.
- Procurement needs the pilot to show baseline SLA + cost telemetry; they want an extra 30 days after the tests to evaluate.
- If we can get a small promotional discount on pilot rates (first month only), it materially helps the CFO sign-off.

I’ve attached the recent usage snapshot so you can see our token profile. Would you be able to escalate for ~200k credits + a 45-day extension and a 20% pilot discount for month 1? That combination would let us run the tests and keep the program moving while procurement finishes their review.

Thanks — happy to jump on a quick call.
Tom
Tom Richards
Head of Product, Velori Analytics
tom.richards@velori.ai | +1-212-555-0168

Attachment: velori_trial_usage_report.pdf (attachment stub)

On Tue, May 19, 2026 at 9:03 AM Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Tom / Sara,
> 
> Thanks again for taking the demo last week — loved seeing the billing-reconciliation use case you built on top of Redwood...


From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 19 May 2026 13:05:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Velori — request for trial top-up + credits (exec approval)

Marissa / Ben — forwarding Velori's ask. Highlights: customer requests ~200k additional free token credits, a 45-day trial extension, and a 20% promo on month 1. They supplied a usage snapshot (attached) showing a bursty profile — most traffic concentrated in a few long sequences.

Why I think it's worth approving (business case):
- Velori is already engaged with two product teams; if pilot succeeds this is a high-propensity expansion into a paid production integration (our AE forecast: $75k ARR in year 1).
- The cost delta for us at our typical quantization/batching will be modest relative to the ACV upside — credits and a small promo de-risk the procurement calendar.
- They are open to a short MSA addendum and ROI metric commitments (we can gate the discount on completed integration tests).

Recommended exception (what I propose we ask execs to approve):
- 150,000 token credits (immediate) + ability to grant another 50k after integration milestone
- 30-day extension now; conditional 15-day extension after usage review (effectively 45 total if milestone met)
- 15% pilot discount for month 1, conditioned on signed addendum with success metrics

Risks / controls:
- Condition credits / promo on signed addendum and a POC success checklist (we should use the attached onboarding checklist + a short integration plan).
- Enable per-account burn alerts at 70% and 90% so we don't overspend.

Can you confirm if this is approvable? If yes I’ll communicate to the customer and get the DocuSign addendum started. Attaching the usage report they sent and a proposed MAP (doc) for the addendum.

Thanks,
Alex

Attachment: velori_trial_usage_report.pdf, proposed_map.docx


From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 20 May 2026 08:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Velori — request for trial top-up + credits (exec approval)

Alex / Ben — thanks for the summary. Approve with conditions.

Approved package (executive sign-off):
- 150,000 token credits to be applied immediately
- 30-day extension to the trial start date; discretionary 15-day extension to be granted only after we validate the integration milestone (i.e., their tests run and we see the telemetry described)
- 15% promotional discount for month 1 of the pilot (not combinable with other promos)

Conditions and next steps:
1) Get a signed addendum (I’ll approve the language in the proposed_map.docx) that includes: defined success metrics, a data retention note, and a termination clause.
2) Set up automatic burn alerts at 70% and 90% of the credit pool; escrow the second 50k behind milestone delivery.
3) Legal and CS should be looped if there are any additional security/compliance asks from Velori.
4) Deadline: customer must sign addendum and confirm integration plan by 2026-06-15 or the credit extension will revert to 30 days only.

Ben — please action the DocuSign addendum, configure the burn alerts in the console, and confirm when credits are loaded. Alex — send the confirmation to Tom/Sara and outline the conditions clearly.

Marissa Cole
VP, Sales — MidMarket, Redwood Inference
marissa_cole@redwood.com | (415) 555-0170



From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Tom Richards <tom.richards@velori.ai>, Sara Liu <sara@velori.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 20 May 2026 16:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Velori — request for trial top-up + credits (exec approval)

Tom / Sara — good news: leadership has approved a partial onramp exception. Summary of what we can do right now (please read closely):

- 150,000 free token credits applied immediately to your account.
- 30-day trial extension granted now. An additional 15-day extension will be granted after we validate the integration milestone (you must confirm completion and we must observe the expected telemetry).
- 15% promotional discount for month 1 of the pilot (applies after extension and only with a signed addendum).

Next steps for you:
1) Please review and sign the addendum I’ve uploaded here: https://drive.redwood.com/shared/velori-proposal (I’ll also push DocuSign in the next hour).
2) Share a short integration test plan and expected token burn window (we need to validate the milestone to release the extra 50k and the discretionary 15-day extension).
3) I’ve CC'd Ben — he’ll load the initial 150k credits and configure 70%/90% burn alerts once the addendum is signed.

Ben / Jonas — I’ll coordinate the DocuSign. Ben — when the addendum is signed please confirm credit load and alert thresholds in this thread.

Thanks all — looking forward to moving this over the finish line.

Alex Martinez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
alex@redwood.com | (415) 555-0124

Related links: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/1234567/deal/VEL-2026-045
Attachments referenced: velori_trial_usage_report.pdf, proposed_map.docx

title: Gated delivery hold — Moscone inbound appointment and reconsignment needed
preview:
From: Moscone Center Freight Ops <freight@moscone.org>
To: CrossPort Logistics <operations@crossportlogistics.com>, Pacific Drayage - Dispatch <dispatch@pacificdrayage.com>
Cc: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: HOLD: Gated delivery - crate ASN mismatch / missing appointment form

Hi team,

We received two inbound crates for Redwood Inference (ASN #RX-77821) but the manifest on-site doesn't match the shipment IDs in our system. Per Moscone inbound policy we cannot accept gated deliveries without a completed appointment form and matching packing list. We placed the shipment on a temporary hold.

Please send the corrected appointment form and updated manifest. If the truck will be reconsigned to our advance warehouse, we need that confirmation and carrier ETA.

Freight ops will release once paperwork is reconciled and appointment is scheduled.

— Moscone Freight Ops
Moscone Convention Center
freight@moscone.org
(415) 555-0123

Attachment: inbound_checklist.pdf (stub)

From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>
To: CrossPort Logistics <operations@crossportlogistics.com>, Pacific Drayage - Dispatch <dispatch@pacificdrayage.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 08:46:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: HOLD: Gated delivery - crate ASN mismatch / missing appointment form

Thanks for the alert — copying our logistics leads.

CrossPort / Pacific — can you confirm which ASN you have on file and whether the carrier has the Moscone appointment form completed? We are seeing ASN RX-77821 and RX-77822 outbounded from our vendor, but Moscone is showing one of the crate IDs as unrecognized. If needed, please reconsign to Moscone Advance Warehouse (address on appointment form) and confirm ETA.

Marissa / Tessa — I’m asking the carriers to send corrected docs. If the crates are held overnight, please prepare the show-site kit (floor samples + one demo unit) to hand-carry in case Moscone only admits a partial unload.

Alex Martinez
Field Marketing Ops | Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com
(650) 555-0199

Attachments: booth_crates_manifest_v3.xlsx

From: CrossPort Logistics <operations@crossportlogistics.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Pacific Drayage - Dispatch <dispatch@pacificdrayage.com>, Moscone Center Freight Ops <freight@moscone.org>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 09:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: HOLD: Gated delivery - crate ASN mismatch / missing appointment form

Alex — thanks, we pulled the POD and manifest from the carrier. The truck shows ASN RX-77821 but crate labels were printed with the vendor's internal PO (PO-9921) which didn't map to the manifest Moscone expects. Dispatch flagged the missing appointment form and attempted to upload it but the Moscone portal rejected it due to a field mismatch (carrier SCAC vs registered SCAC).

Immediate next steps we can take:
- Re-submit the correct appointment form (we need the Moscone form filled by Redwood; please see attached form you can sign),
- If Moscone requires reconsignment to Advance Warehouse we can complete that tonight (additional drayage fee estimate: $420) and provide a new ETA for parking and unloading,
- Or we can have the truck return to cross-dock for relabeling and then re-deliver first thing tomorrow.

Please advise which option you prefer. We will also request Moscone to release one crate if they will accept a partial unload with on-site kit.

— Javier Ortiz
Operations Manager, CrossPort Logistics
operations@crossportlogistics.com
Attachment: moscone_appointment_form_fillable.pdf

From: GDS Customs Broker <customs@gds-broker.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 11:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: HOLD: Gated delivery - commercial invoice update needed

Alex — I checked the shipment documentation. Commercial Invoice for RX-77822 shows "Redwood Inf" as consignee; Moscone requires full legal name on commercial paperwork to process bonded entries. Please upload an updated commercial invoice that matches Redwood's legal entity and the manifest. Without this we may face additional customs hold time if cargo is reconsigned.

I can prepare the corrected invoice if you provide: final declared value per crate and the Redwood legal entity line (we typically use Redwood Inference, Inc.).

— Priya Shah
GDS Customs Broker
customs@gds-broker.com
(415) 555-0456

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: CrossPort Logistics <operations@crossportlogistics.com>, GDS Customs Broker <customs@gds-broker.com>, Moscone Center Freight Ops <freight@moscone.org>
Cc: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 13:20:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: HOLD: Gated delivery - commercial invoice update needed

Thanks Priya / CrossPort — attaching updated commercial invoice (filled) and the Moscone appointment form completed with our SCAC. We elected to reconsign to Moscone Advance Warehouse to avoid in-venue delays; CrossPort confirmed pickup tonight and will deliver to the advance location for unloading. CrossPort — please provide the new AWB/ASN or tracking ID and the estimated arrival window.

Tessa / Rafael — Rafael, can you confirm the on-site kit readiness in case Moscone only releases one crate tomorrow? Tessa, Rafael — please coordinate one-man quick install for the demo unit if needed.

Marissa Cole
Sr. Events Program Manager | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com
(650) 555-0222

Attachments: commercial_invoice_redwood.pdf, moscone_appointment_form.pdf, floorplan_moscone_level2.pdf

From: CrossPort Logistics <operations@crossportlogistics.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Moscone Center Freight Ops <freight@moscone.org>, Pacific Drayage - Dispatch <dispatch@pacificdrayage.com>
Date: Wed, 20 May 2026 16:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Delivery update: reconsigned to Moscone Advance Warehouse / appointment set for 05/21 07:00

Marissa / Alex — update: crates RX-77821/RX-77822 were reconsigned to Moscone Advance Warehouse. New tracking: MP-554900. ETA at advance warehouse: 05/21 07:00-09:00. Moscone confirmed appointment for in-hall delivery 05/22 between 06:30-09:30 (gate code provided in appointment PDF). Fees for reconsignment + warehousing: $420 (one-time).

Moscone also confirmed they will allow a one-crate partial release on 05/21 for the on-site kit if Marissa confirms the kit list and shows signed floorplan on arrival. Rafael — please plan to pick up the partial release and move to the demo spot if released early; otherwise we are on track for full delivery on 05/22 morning.

— Javier Ortiz
Operations Manager, CrossPort Logistics
operations@crossportlogistics.com

Attachment: moscone_appointment_confirm_05-21.pdf

title: Joint billing & SKU mapping for marketplace bundled offer
preview:
From: Rajesh Menon <rajesh@dataforge.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 19 May 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: SKU mapping for bundled offer (initial)

Hi Ben / team,

Thanks again for the quick demo yesterday. We heard positive feedback from our GTM and pricing teams and want to move toward a small pilot listing on AWS marketplace where our DataForge analytics bundle will be packaged with Redwood inference credits.

To keep procurement simple for pilot customers, AWS has asked for a clear SKU mapping and a recommended billing flow (who invoices what, and how credits are redeemed). I attached an initial CSV with our SKU names (df-analytics-core, df-analytics-pro) — can your ops team map these to Redwood SKU patterns and confirm whether a bundled private-offer is feasible?

Key asks:
- Confirm Redwood SKU equivalents and suggested metering labels
- Clarify who issues the invoice for the bundle during pilot (DataForge vs Redwood)
- Any known constraints for private-offer pricing and co-billing on AWS

We can be flexible on commercial terms for the pilot (short term credits + usage sharing).

Attaching df_sku_map_v1.csv for reference.

Best,
Rajesh
Partnerships, DataForge

Attachments: df_sku_map_v1.csv (text/csv)

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Rajesh Menon <rajesh@dataforge.com>

Subject: Re: SKU mapping for bundled offer (initial)

Rajesh — thanks, this looks promising.

High level from our side:
- We can publish a bundled listing where the DataForge SKU is visible and Redwood provides a consumption credit SKU that decrements usage. The mapping will need explicit metering dimensions for: inference_token_hours, embedding_units, and premium-support-hours (if included).
- For the pilot, easiest path is DataForge invoices the customer and redeems Redwood credits via a private-offer that we provision to DataForge-managed customers. That keeps billing and legal simpler. We can explore co-billing later.

Vivek / Monica / Karthik — can you take point on:
  1) producing a canonical SKU map and proposed metering labels (CSV)
  2) confirming any AWS private-offer constraints we need to flag to DataForge
  3) sizing an initial credit allocation and how we track usage for revenue share

I attached a short pricing note as background (redwood-df-pricing-notes.pdf). Rajesh — can you confirm target pilot region(s) and sample customers?

Thanks,
Ben
Head of Partnerships, Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood-df-pricing-notes.pdf (application/pdf)

From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Rajesh Menon <rajesh@dataforge.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 19 May 2026 13:18:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: SKU mapping for bundled offer (initial)

Couple of technical/ops points after a quick scan:
- Metering: we prefer to expose a single metering dimension for the Redwood credit (redwood_inference_credits) and let DataForge report usage in terms of credits consumed per workflow. That reduces complexity in the marketplace listing.
- SKU IDs: our catalog requires a stable SKU id (alphanumeric) and a human-friendly display name. I'll generate the catalog-ready CSV and include mapping to the df_sku_map_v1.csv fields.
- Private-offer process: AWS requires the PO to reference the private-offer id; our procurement team can sign a reseller agreement or we can provide credits directly to DataForge under a reseller arrangement.

I'll have the SKU mapping draft by EOD Thu (5/21). Monica — can you confirm any finance/contract redlines we should expect if Redwood is on the invoice?

-Vivek
Senior Partnerships Manager

title: Clarify scan tempo & CVE remediation tracking for Pegasus SOC2 review
Reserved GPU pool — capacity definitions & SLA language
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Lisa Jordan <lisa.jordan@acme.health>
Cc: Andrew Cho <andrew.cho@acme.health>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Dedicated Reserved Pricing - initial proposal and clarifications

Hi Lisa / Andrew,

Thanks for the time last week. Attached is the proposal for a 3x V100-equivalent reserved GPU pool sized for Acme's expected production peak: 48 vGPU-equivalents (equivalent to 6 x 8-GPU hosts in our SKU model). The PDF includes pricing tiers, optional burst credits, and baseline throughput guarantees.

Quick highlights:
- Reserved commit: 12 months, billed monthly.
- Base throughput SLO: 99th pct token latency <= 150ms for 1–8 concurrent streams at 2k context (see proposal pg. 3).
- Capacity unit definition: our SKU uses \"GPU-capacity units (GCU)\" where 1 GCU = 1 x 80GB A100 equivalent slot. We provide quantified mapping to common family/models in the appendix.
- Discount: we can offer a 22% volume discount on committed spend for the 12-month term; additional discounts possible on multi-year.

A couple items I want to clarify up front so your procurement/legal review is smoother:
1) How do you want us to define \"available capacity\" for burst vs committed (we have standard language but happy to align).
2) Any must-have SLA remedies you require beyond service credits (e.g., termination rights, additional credits for multi-day outages)?

Attachment: RI-Dedicated-Pricing-Proposal-ACME.pdf (proposal)

Next steps: if the pricing looks close, would you like us to draft a set of contract redlines focused solely on the pricing & SLA sections for review? Karthik and Marissa from our side will support legal language.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter | Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0193

---
Redwood Inference — production-first inference for engineering teams


From: Lisa Jordan <lisa.jordan@acme.health>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Andrew Cho <andrew.cho@acme.health>, Tom Baker <tbaker@acme.legal>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Dedicated Reserved Pricing - initial proposal and clarifications

Ben — thanks for the proposal. A few clarifying questions from procurement and our infra team before we loop legal:

1) Capacity unit mapping: can you provide the explicit mapping table for common GPUs (A100 80GB, A100 40GB, L40s, V100) to your GCU? Our infra wants to understand headroom sizing.

2) Burst capacity: how is burst billed if we exceed committed GCUs for short spikes? Is it per-minute, hourly, and is there a cap? Also, what does \"available capacity\" mean operationally (e.g., queued requests vs throttled)?

3) SLA/service credits: we typically ask for a more prescriptive credit schedule tied to monthly availability percentage and a cap. Also need to confirm whether credits can be applied to future invoices or must be cash refunds.

4) Definitions: we need a clear, contract-level definition of \"Capacity Unit\", \"Throughput Guarantee\", and \"Baseline Model Variant\" (because some of our traffic can switch model families).

Finally, please have your contracts lead prepare redline suggestions for the dedicated section — we prefer to receive proposed language rather than open-ended back-and-forth.

Regards,
Lisa Jordan
Head of Procurement, Acme Health
lisa.jordan@acme.health


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Lisa Jordan <lisa.jordan@acme.health>
Cc: Andrew Cho <andrew.cho@acme.health>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 14:21:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Dedicated Reserved Pricing - initial proposal and clarifications

Hi Lisa — great questions. Short answers here; we'll follow up with precise contract language. Looping legal/infra items into a single doc per your ask.

1) Mapping: 1 GCU = 1 x A100-80GB equivalent. For reference: A100-40GB = 0.55 GCU, L40s = 0.9 GCU (approx), V100 = 0.35 GCU. We'll attach a table with perf/throughput numbers per model family for your capacity planning.

2) Burst billing: bursts are billed by the hour at the \"on-demand\" per-GCU rate, with automatic aggregation per calendar day and a daily cap equal to 20% of committed GCUs unless you opt into a higher burst cap. Operationally we consider \"available capacity\" the unreserved pool that can be scheduled on-demand; requests beyond that are queued for up to 30s before failing or routing to a fallback (configurable).

3) SLA credits: standard is service credits applied to future invoices (not cash refunds). Typical schedule: 5% credit for monthly availability 99.0–99.9%, 15% credit for 95.0–98.9%, 50% credit for <95.0%, capped at 100% of monthly committed invoice. If you require cash refunds we can escalate to legal but that is sensitive.

4) Definitions: agree — we'll propose contract text defining Capacity Unit (GCU), Throughput Guarantee (measured as 99th token latency for specified concurrency/context), and Baseline Model Variant (named model family pinned for SLOs).

I'll get Karthik and Marissa to draft redlines with the concrete language you can feed to legal.

Thanks,
Ben


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Lisa Jordan <lisa.jordan@acme.health>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Tom Baker <tbaker@acme.legal>
Date: Tue, 19 May 2026 10:08:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Reserved GPU pool — capacity definitions & SLA language

Hi Lisa — Karthik here (Sales Lead). Thanks for the clear list. Marissa (our contracts lead) is on copy and will propose redline language focused on: (A) precise GCU definition and mapping table, (B) burst billing mechanics and caps, (C) SLA credit schedule and cap, and (D) a compromise on termination/exit treatment tied to sustained under-delivery.

Marissa — action: please prepare a tidy redline we can attach back to Acme Legal. Ben, please include the performance mapping table.

Lisa/Acme team — plan to send the redlines by EOD tomorrow (20 May). If you have any must-have contract elements beyond what you listed, flag them now.

Best,
Karthik


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Lisa Jordan <lisa.jordan@acme.health>, Tom Baker <tbaker@acme.legal>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 20 May 2026 09:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Draft redlines — Dedicated pricing, capacity unit, and SLA credits (attached)

Hi Lisa / Tom — attaching proposed redlines focused only on the Dedicated Pricing and SLA sections to keep review narrow: ACME_Dedicated_Redlines_20260520.docx (attached). Key suggested language below. Please consider these as starting points — we are open to refinement.

1) Capacity Unit (contract clause suggestion):
\"Capacity Unit (\"GCU\"): A GCU denotes a reserved capacity increment equivalent to one 80GB A100 GPU. Redwood will publish a mapping table (Schedule X) that maps common GPU types to GCUs for purposes of sizing and billing. The mapping table may be updated by Redwood with 30 days' notice; updates do not reduce prior committed capacity.

2) Throughput Guarantee (contract clause suggestion):
\"Throughput Guarantee\": For each billing period, Redwood guarantees that the 99th percentile per-token generation latency for traffic routed to a committed GCU pool will not exceed 150 milliseconds for requests matching the Baseline Model Variant, up to the concurrency level specified in Schedule Y. Service credits are available per the SLA Credit Schedule below.

3) Burst and Over‑commit Billing (contract clause suggestion):
\"Burst Capacity\": Customer may consume on-demand capacity above committed GCUs. On-demand usage will be billed at the published on-demand per-GCU hourly rate, aggregated hourly and capped daily at 20% of the committed GCUs unless Customer elects a higher cap in writing. Redwood will queue excess requests for up to thirty (30) seconds before routing to fallback model or returning an error, per configuration.

4) SLA Credit Schedule (contract clause suggestion):
- Monthly availability >= 99.90%: 0% credit
- Monthly availability >= 99.00% and < 99.90%: 5% credit of monthly committed invoice
- Monthly availability >= 95.00% and < 99.00%: 15% credit
- Monthly availability < 95.00%: 50% credit
Credits shall be applied to Customer's subsequent invoices and are capped at 100% of the monthly committed invoice. Credit is Customer's sole and exclusive remedy for availability failures except for Redwood's gross negligence or willful misconduct.

5) Termination for sustained under-delivery (compromise):
If Redwood fails to meet the Throughput Guarantee for three (3) consecutive months, Customer may provide written notice. Redwood will have thirty (30) days to cure. If not cured, Customer may terminate the Dedicated Pricing Schedule and receive a pro-rata refund of pre-paid committed fees for future periods.

Please review the attached doc for line-by-line edits. We have kept the redlines narrow to avoid reopening unrelated commercial terms.

Attachment: ACME_Dedicated_Redlines_20260520.docx

Thanks,
Marissa Cole | Senior Contracts Manager, Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.com


From: Tom Baker <tbaker@acme.legal>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Lisa Jordan <lisa.jordan@acme.health>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 20 May 2026 15:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Draft redlines — Dedicated pricing, capacity unit, and SLA credits (attached)

Marissa — thanks for the focused draft. A few items from legal: we prefer credits be available as either invoice credit or, upon Customer election, as a cash refund where the credit exceeds 50% of the monthly invoice. Also request explicit language that mapping table changes cannot be used to retroactively increase Customer's committed GCU obligations.

Proposed minor tweak: add a clause that if Redwood changes the mapping table in a way that increases the GCU equivalence for a pinned Baseline Model Variant within the term, Redwood will honor the original equivalence for the remainder of the then-current committed term for purposes of billing and SLA.

If acceptable, we can sign off and route to procurement for final countersign.

Tom Baker
Senior Counsel, Acme Health


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Tom Baker <tbaker@acme.legal>, Lisa Jordan <lisa.jordan@acme.health>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 20 May 2026 16:10:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Draft redlines — Dedicated pricing, capacity unit, and SLA credits (attached)

Tom — appreciate the quick review. We can accept the alternate credit payment election (credit or cash refund where credits exceed 50%), and we'll add the non-retroactivity clause for mapping-table increases as you suggested. I'll produce the updated redline and send for countersign by EOD.

Marissa

3) SLA/service credits: we typically ask for a more prescriptive credit schedule tied to monthly availability percentage and a cap. Also need to confirm whether credits can be applied to future invoices or must be cash refunds.

4) Definitions: we need a clear, contract-level definition of \"Capacity Unit\", \"Throughput Guarantee\", and \"Baseline Model Variant\" (because some of our traffic can switch model families).

Finally, please have your contracts lead prepare redline suggestions for the dedicated section — we prefer to receive proposed language rather than open-ended back-and-forth.

Regards,
Lisa Jordan
Head of Procurement, Acme Health
lisa.jordan@acme.health


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Lisa Jordan <lisa.jordan@acme.health>
Cc: Andrew Cho <andrew.cho@acme.health>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 14:21:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Dedicated Reserved Pricing - initial proposal and clarifications

Hi Lisa — great questions. Short answers here; we'll follow up with precise contract language. Looping legal/infra items into a single doc per your ask.

1) Mapping: 1 GCU = 1 x A100-80GB equivalent. For reference: A100-40GB = 0.55 GCU, L40s = 0.9 GCU (approx), V100 = 0.35 GCU. We'll attach a table with perf/throughput numbers per model family for your capacity planning.

2) Burst billing: bursts are billed by the hour at the \"on-demand\" per-GCU rate, with automatic aggregation per calendar day and a daily cap equal to 20% of committed GCUs unless you opt into a higher burst cap. Operationally we consider \"available capacity\" the unreserved pool that can be scheduled on-demand; requests beyond that are queued for up to 30s before failing or routing to a fallback (configurable).

3) SLA credits: standard is service credits applied to future invoices (not cash refunds). Typical schedule: 5% credit for monthly availability 99.0–99.9%, 15% credit for 95.0–98.9%, 50% credit for <95.0%, capped at 100% of monthly committed invoice. If you require cash refunds we can escalate to legal but that is sensitive.

4) Definitions: agree — we'll propose contract text defining Capacity Unit (GCU), Throughput Guarantee (measured as 99th token latency for specified concurrency/context), and Baseline Model Variant (named model family pinned for SLOs).

I'll get Karthik and Marissa to draft redlines with the concrete language you can feed to legal.

Thanks,
Ben

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Lisa Jordan <lisa.jordan@acme.health>, Tom Baker <tbaker@acme.legal>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 20 May 2026 09:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Draft redlines — Dedicated pricing, capacity unit, and SLA credits (attached)

Hi Lisa / Tom — attaching proposed redlines focused only on the Dedicated Pricing and SLA sections to keep review narrow: ACME_Dedicated_Redlines_20260520.docx (attached). Key suggested language below. Please consider these as starting points — we are open to refinement.

1) Capacity Unit (contract clause suggestion):
\"Capacity Unit (\"GCU\"): A GCU denotes a reserved capacity increment equivalent to one 80GB A100 GPU. Redwood will publish a mapping table (Schedule X) that maps common GPU types to GCUs for purposes of sizing and billing. The mapping table may be updated by Redwood with 30 days' notice; updates do not reduce prior committed capacity.

2) Throughput Guarantee (contract clause suggestion):
\"Throughput Guarantee\": For each billing period, Redwood guarantees that the 99th percentile per-token generation latency for traffic routed to a committed GCU pool will not exceed 150 milliseconds for requests matching the Baseline Model Variant, up to the concurrency level specified in Schedule Y. Service credits are available per the SLA Credit Schedule below.

3) Burst and Over‑commit Billing (contract clause suggestion):
\"Burst Capacity\": Customer may consume on-demand capacity above committed GCUs. On-demand usage will be billed at the published on-demand per-GCU hourly rate, aggregated hourly and capped daily at 20% of the committed GCUs unless Customer elects a higher cap in writing. Redwood will queue excess requests for up to thirty (30) seconds before routing to fallback model or returning an error, per configuration.

4) SLA Credit Schedule (contract clause suggestion):
- Monthly availability >= 99.90%: 0% credit
- Monthly availability >= 99.00% and < 99.90%: 5% credit of monthly committed invoice
- Monthly availability >= 95.00% and < 99.00%: 15% credit
- Monthly availability < 95.00%: 50% credit
Credits shall be applied to Customer's subsequent invoices and are capped at 100% of the monthly committed invoice. Credit is Customer's sole and exclusive remedy for availability failures except for Redwood's gross negligence or willful misconduct.

5) Termination for sustained under-delivery (compromise):
If Redwood fails to meet the Throughput Guarantee for three (3) consecutive months, Customer may provide written notice. Redwood will have thirty (30) days to cure. If not cured, Customer may terminate the Dedicated Pricing Schedule and receive a pro-rata refund of pre-paid committed fees for future periods.

Please review the attached doc for line-by-line edits. We have kept the redlines narrow to avoid reopening unrelated commercial terms.

Attachment: ACME_Dedicated_Redlines_20260520.docx

Thanks,
Marissa Cole | Senior Contracts Manager, Redwood Inference

title: Custody cutover matrix + ops asks for Dedicated vs Private VPC
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Samira Khan <samira.k@mediquant.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Custody cutover matrix + ops asks for Dedicated vs Private VPC

Samira — thanks again for the time this morning. Quick recap and three asks so we can scope the next PoC accurately.

Recap (from our notes):
- Primary concern: complete logical separation of customer data and model context (PHI & billing-sensitive IDs)
- MediQuant prefers control over KMS and key rotation policy; they are sensitive to any Redwood-managed plaintext persistence
- Throughput targets: sustained 300 reqs/min with 512-token avg; latency SLOs are 95th < 250ms for priority routes
- Ops expectations: audit logs for request traces, access audit for keys, and an isolated control plane preferred

Three asks:
1) Can you confirm whether Dedicated capacity (Redwood-managed GPUs with tenant isolation) would be acceptable if we hand over KMS/HSM control and guarantee no shared persistent storage?
2) If not, can you specify the minimum network connectivity model for Private VPC (e.g., customer-managed NAT, egress controls, or fully air-gapped on-prem)?
3) Please attach any compliance evidence you need us to provide in the PoC (SOC2 scope items, encryption-at-rest proofs, or any specific logs).

Attached: redwood-custody-matrix-v1.xlsx (draft) — shows where responsibilities fall between Hosted / Dedicated / Private for keys, logs, and model artifacts. Also attached SOC2_Audit_Summary.pdf for your security team.

Next steps I propose:
- You reply with preferred option (Dedicated w/ customer KMS vs Private VPC) and any dealbreakers
- If Private VPC, we’ll schedule a 60-min network/ops call with our infra engineer (Monica) and your security lead to walk the cutover matrix
- If Dedicated, we’ll produce an ops runbook showing how keys and ephemeral caches are isolated and run a connectivity test during PoC prep

Thanks — Ben

--
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0123



From: Samira Khan <samira.k@mediquant.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Daniel Lee <dan.lee@mediquant.com>, security@mediquant.com, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 11:08:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Custody cutover matrix + ops asks for Dedicated vs Private VPC

Ben — appreciate the crisp recap. A few clarifications from our side after reviewing the matrix:

1) KMS ownership: must be customer-managed keys for data-at-rest AND request-token logging. We can evaluate a model where Redwood uses a customer KMS endpoint (BYOK) but it must be in our cloud account (not just keys exported).

2) Persistent artifacts: no model shards or checkpoints may be written to shared network filesystems with multi-tenant residency. Ephemeral caches on the GPU are OK if flushed and not checkpointed to remote storage.

3) Control plane: we prefer control-plane residency within our VPC for auditability; however we are open to a split model where the control plane is in Redwood but with an API gateway that logs every control operation to our SIEM via syslog/HTTPS.

4) Timeline & PoC scope: prefer a 4-week PoC. Week 1: network & KMS proof-of-concept. Week 2-3: traffic simulation and latency validation. Week 4: security sign-off.

We’ll need the following from Redwood ahead of the PoC kickoff:
- A network diagram describing inbound/outbound ports for Private VPC and Dedicated (hosted) alternatives
- A short runbook showing KMS BYOK integration steps and any prerequisites
- A sample audit log (anonymized) demonstrating request id mapping to token traces

I’ll loop in Daniel (cc) to confirm budget owner; he may ask for an estimate around incremental ops for Private VPC versus Dedicated.

Thanks,
Samira

--
Samira Khan
Head of Platform Security
MediQuant, Inc.
samira.k@mediquant.com | +1 617-555-3344



From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Samira Khan <samira.k@mediquant.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 19 May 2026 09:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Custody cutover matrix + ops asks for Dedicated vs Private VPC

Samira — looping to confirm how we’ll support those deliverables. Monica will own the network diagram and the BYOK runbook; I’ll own the sample audit log and the PoC schedule.

Quick notes on feasibility:
- BYOK into customer cloud account: feasible, we have a standard connector pattern used by other customers. We’ll document required IAM roles and network endpoints.
- Ephemeral-only caches: our runtime can be configured to avoid checkpointing; we’ll include an explicit cache-flush step in the PoC traffic script.
- Control plane split: we can provide a push of all control plane events to an external syslog endpoint; we’ll need the SIEM endpoint and expected format (CEF/JSON).

Action items for Redwood (owners):
- Monica: deliver network diagram + BYOK runbook (target Thu 21 May EOD)
- Marissa: anonymized audit log sample and PoC schedule (target Fri 22 May)
- Ben: prepare a short cost delta estimate for Private VPC vs Dedicated for Daniel (target Mon 25 May)

If any of these dates are tight for you Samira, flag and we’ll adjust. Looking forward to locking the scope.

Best — Marissa

Marissa Cole
Senior Customer Success Manager
Redwood Inference

Date: Thu, 20 May 2026 15:02:00 -07:00

title: ClearSight — technical qualification, PoC next steps & prompt governance
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Julia Park <julia.park@clearsight.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 13:02:00 -0700
Subject: Post-demo recap + technical qualification / next steps

Hi Julia — thanks again for the call this morning. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we can move to a scoped PoC: 

Summary of needs (from our conversation):
- SDK + auth: short path for developer onboarding (API keys + short-lived OIDC tokens), want examples for both hosted API and private VPC setups.
- Routing & capacity: route inference by region and cost tier (low-latency models vs cheaper fallbacks).
- Structured output / tool calling: primary use case is invoice extraction + multi-step verification (tool call to internal validation service).
- Embeddings + reranker: semantic search over historical invoices; want a candidate-ranking reranker layer.
- Prompt governance: versioned system prompts, mitigations for prompt injection, and a red-team review cadence.

Proposed next steps:
1) I attached a short PoC outline (redwood_poc_outline.pdf) that maps milestones: discovery, fast prototype (2 weeks), evaluation, and rollout.
2) Share Clearsight integration diagram and security contacts (we'll need your sec/compliance POC).
3) We'll prepare: sample SDK snippets (Node/Python), example system prompt templates, and a security brief (KMS/retention/audit logs).
4) Kickoff call w/ Eng + Security next week — propose Tue May 25 or Wed May 26 (30–45m).

Notes on prompt governance (high level):
- We recommend storing system prompts in a version-controlled artifact (Git or Redwood Console prompt registry).
- Use signature/version headers on prompts (e.g., prompt_v2026-05-18) and keep immutable snapshots tied to eval suites.
- For injection risk: canonicalize user input, enforce token limits for user-provided content, and apply a pre-inference sanitizer policy (we can share examples).
- Red-team: run regular adversarial prompt batches and measure policy violations; we can offer one red-team workshop during the PoC.

Attachments: redwood_poc_outline.pdf

Open questions for you (so we can scope effort):
- Do you need a private VPC with BYOK for the PoC or is hosted API fine to start?
- Any minimum SLA/throughput targets for invoice extraction?

I’ll loop in Marissa and Steph on pricing and security details. If those dates work, I’ll send a calendar invite and the initial SOW.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0142

--- Attachment: redwood_poc_outline.pdf (1 page — PoC milestones + scope)

To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 16:14:00 -07:00

- Security/compliance: I’m copying our security mailbox (security@clearsight.ai) — they’ll lead the questionnaire. Key asks upfront: data residency (do you support US-only regions for hosted?), BYOK/KMS options, and audited access logs for admin/API keys.
- PoC scope: we can start with hosted API for a faster turn, but will likely require private VPC for production. For PoC, can you confirm encryption-at-rest and whether logs contain PII?
- Prompt templates: can you share a couple of sample system prompts + an example of structured output schema for invoice extraction? Our legal team also wants to see any red-team process notes.
- Pricing: rough estimate for a 2-week PoC (engineering/infra + support) so I can float to procurement.

Attaching our integration map (clearsight_integration_map.pdf) which shows our validation service and where we’d like tool-calls.

Thanks — I’m available Tue/Wed afternoons for the kickoff.

— Julia

Head of Product | ClearSight Analytics
julia.park@clearsight.ai

> On Mon, May 18, 2026 at 1:02 PM Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com> wrote:
> > [original message omitted for brevity]

--- Attachment: clearsight_integration_map.pdf (diagram, 2 pages)

From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Julia Park <julia@clearsight.ai>, security@clearsight.ai
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 19 May 2026 09:37:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo recap + technical qualification / next steps

Hi Julia / ClearSight Security — looping in short answers and tangible artifacts so your team can triage: 

Security & data residency:
- Regions: Hosted API supports region pinning; we can restrict processing to US regions for the PoC.
- KMS/BYOK: Supported. We integrate with AWS KMS for BYOK in both hosted and private deployments; HSM options available for on-prem.
- Audit logs: Admin and API key activity logs are available; we can provide daily ingestion to your SIEM or share S3 exports.
- PII in logs: Sensitive payloads can be redacted before logging; we provide controls to disable detailed request bodies in logs.

Private deployment vs hosted for PoC:
- Hosted: quickest to start (days). Comes with out-of-the-box SDKs and example prompts.
- Private / VPC: needed for production if you require network isolation/BYOK; provisioning timeline ~2–4 weeks depending on infra approvals.

Prompt governance artifacts (attached: prompt_policy_v1.md) — highlights:
- Version control: prompts stored as immutable artifacts with a version header and changelog (example: system_prompt_v1.2 — see file).
- Injection mitigations: 1) canonicalize and whitelist structured fields; 2) run static heuristics to detect prompt-like tokens; 3) prepend a sanitized system prompt that enforces allowed actions and denies arbitrary tool invocations.
- Red-team: PoC includes one-week adversarial prompt run against the prompt set; we’ll report safety regressions and tune templates.

Sample structured output (invoice extraction):
- Output schema (JSON): {"invoice_id":"str","vendor":"str","total_amount":{"value":float,"currency":"str"},"line_items":[{"description":"str","quantity":int,"unit_price":float}],"confidence":float}
- For tool-calling we recommend: 
  1) Model returns structured JSON according to schema.
  2) Redwood runtime validates schema server-side and rejects malformed outputs.
  3) If confidence < threshold, call validation tool with original payload for human review.

Attachments: redwood_security_overview.pdf, prompt_policy_v1.md, sample_sdk_snippets.zip (we will send SDK snippets in the drive link).

- KMS/BYOK: Supported. We integrate with AWS KMS for BYOK in both hosted and private deployments; HSM options available for on-prem.
- Audit logs: Admin and API key activity logs are available; we can provide daily ingestion to your SIEM or share S3 exports.

Private deployment vs hosted for PoC:
- Hosted: quickest to start (days). Comes with out-of-the-box SDKs and example prompts.
- Private / VPC: needed for production if you require network isolation/BYOK; provisioning timeline ~2–4 weeks depending on infra approvals.

Prompt governance artifacts (attached: prompt_policy_v1.md) — highlights:
- Version control: prompts stored as immutable artifacts with a version header and changelog (example: system_prompt_v1.2 — see file).
- Injection mitigations: 1) canonicalize and whitelist structured fields; 2) run static heuristics to detect prompt-like tokens; 3) prepend a sanitized system prompt that enforces allowed actions and denies arbitrary tool invocations.
- Red-team: PoC includes one-week adversarial prompt run against the prompt set; we’ll report safety regressions and tune templates.

Sample structured output (invoice extraction):
- Output schema (JSON): {"invoice_id":"str","vendor":"str","total_amount":{"value":float,"currency":"str"},"line_items":[{"description":"str","quantity":int,"unit_price":float}],"confidence":float}
- For tool-calling we recommend: 
  1) Model returns structured JSON according to schema.
  2) Redwood runtime validates schema server-side and rejects malformed outputs.
  3) If confidence < threshold, call validation tool with original payload for human review.

title: Step-up cadence proposal for Dedicated capacity — StrataGen
preview:
From: Catherine Thomas <catherine.thomas@stratagen.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Sales Ops <salesops@stratagen.com>
Date: Sun, 17 May 2026 09:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity — long-term commitment structure

Ben,

Thanks again for the demo last week — your latency and cost profile looks like a great fit for our product team. We’ve started internal budgeting and want to understand a structured multi-year prepay that lets us grow without paying for idle GPUs early. High level: 3-year contract, but we want an annualized prepay with a step-up cadence: year1 = 30% of committed TPM/GPU, year2 = 50%, year3 = 100%.

Questions:
- Can Redwood offer a term discount tied to the cumulative committed capacity rather than frontloading?
- If we adopt a step-up cadence, what are assumed fill/usage expectations and credits if we miss year milestones?
- Any flexibility on invoice timing (annual vs quarterly) — our procurement prefers annual prepay but can consider blended schedules.

Can you send a simple economics bake showing prices per TPM and effective discount at each year? We’re targeting procurement sign-off by end of next week.

Catherine
Catherine Thomas | Head of Cloud Ops, StrataGen
[Attachment: annual_commit_template.xlsx]


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Catherine Thomas <catherine.thomas@stratagen.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Step-up cadence proposal for Dedicated capacity — StrataGen

Catherine — appreciate the clarity. I ran a quick pass and attached a step-up schedule with two pricing tracks: (A) fixed annual prepay with year-by-year step-ups and (B) blended annual prepay with an upfront credit applied across year1-year3. Key points up-front:

- Year-by-year step-up (your proposed 30/50/100 split) is doable. We typically model commit discounts on annual committed spend; the deeper the multi-year commit, the higher the cumulative discount.
- We can tie milestone credits: if StrataGen fails to meet Year 1 utilization >= 60% of committed capacity, we offer a one-time credit equal to 10% of that year's payment to apply against Year 2 invoice (instead of refunds).
- Invoice timing: we can do annual prepay (preferred for committed discount), or split into two semi-annual invoices for your treasury cadence.

I attached stepup_schedule_v2.xlsx which shows per-TPM rates and effective blended discount at the account level for a 3-year 40% total commit. Two ask for you: 1) confirm whether your procurement requires amortization over accounting periods (so we can mirror invoicing), and 2) confirm preferred invoice frequency.

If this looks good, I’ll loop in Legal to draft a short exhibit for the step-up cadence and milestone credit language.

Best,
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
[Attachment: stepup_schedule_v2.xlsx]

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 18 May 2026 13:45:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Step-up cadence proposal for Dedicated capacity — StrataGen

Ben — quick internal note before we send the legal exhibit. Forwarding Catherine’s ask and your schedule. A few negotiation levers we should consider to protect gross margin while remaining competitive: 

- Add an explicit annual utilization band for each year (e.g., Year1: 60-85% expected; credits trigger below lower bound, no additional discount above upper bound). This helps limit over-provisioning exposure.
- Make the Year 1 credit apply only once and cap the credit at 15% of Year1 payment if they underutilize severely.
- Tie the deepest discount to a modest acceleration clause: if they front-load usage and move to >120% of Year2 commitments, we offer conversion credit toward on-demand tokens instead of retro refunds.
- Consider offering invoice-term flexibility as a commercial concession (annual vs semi-annual) but hard-limit cancellations — 60 days notice with prorated conversion to on-demand at our published rates.

title: Joint ON24+Zoom webinar: registration mapping & stream fallback plan
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Priya Shah <priya@on24.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie_nguyen@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa_morgan@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 12 May 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Joint ON24+Zoom webinar: registration mapping & stream fallback plan

Hi Priya and team,

We're finalizing the configuration for the Acme Corp webinar on May 25 and I wanted to align on two things before we lock the rehearsal schedule: registration field mapping (ON24 -> HubSpot & Zoom) and the streaming fallback plan if the ON24 encoder hits bitrate limits.

Quick ask: can you confirm the canonical field names we should surface in the ON24 registration form so they map cleanly into HubSpot? We need: full name, email, company, job title, region, and a consent checkbox for marketing. Also, can ON24 pass a persistent registrant_id that we can use as a foreign key in HubSpot?

For streaming: our preference is to stream primarily through ON24 (primary) with Zoom webinar as an overflow/backfill stream. If ON24 encounters encoder bitrate spikes we want an automated fallback to a Zoom RTMP ingest (and record on Zoom). What capabilities does ON24 expose for automatic fallback, and do we need a separate RTMP endpoint from Zoom for this to work?

I've attached the ON24 production checklist and a proposed registration field list.

Thanks — planning to schedule the full tech rehearsal for Friday 5/22.

— Ava
Ava Chen
Events Program Manager, Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.com
Redwood Inference

Attachments: on24-prod-checklist.pdf


From: Priya Shah <priya@on24.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie_nguyen@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa_morgan@redwood.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 12 May 2026 12:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Joint ON24+Zoom webinar: registration mapping & stream fallback plan

Hi Ava — thanks for the detailed note. Short answers below, followed by a recommended config.

1) Registration mapping: ON24 supports custom field API keys and will pass those back in the registration webhook payload. Use these keys: registrant_full_name, registrant_email, registrant_company, registrant_title, registrant_region, marketing_consent. We can also attach a persistent registrant_id in the webhook for matching to HubSpot. We'll send sample webhook payloads after this thread.

2) Fallback streaming: ON24 does not provide an automatic, seamless fallback to a third-party RTMP by default, but we can implement a heartbeat + script-based failover on our encoder layer. Practically that means provision a warm Zoom RTMP ingest endpoint and a small lambda (or ON24 event script) to trigger RTMP redirect when bitrate drops below threshold for X seconds. Yes, Zoom can accept RTMP and record on the Zoom side.

Recommended items (short):
- Provide Zoom RTMP endpoint and backup stream key.
- Agree on bitrate threshold (we suggest 2.5 Mbps for 720p slides + speaker audio).
- Confirm where recordings will live (ON24 archive vs Zoom cloud).
- Confirm webhook endpoint for registration POSTs and sample payload.

I'll attach a short checklist and sample webhook. Priya
ON24 CSM
Priya Shah
priya@on24.com

Attachments: on24-production-checklist.pdf


From: Tessa Morgan <tessa_morgan@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Priya Shah <priya@on24.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 13 May 2026 08:33:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Joint ON24+Zoom webinar: registration mapping & stream fallback plan

Thanks Priya — a couple clarifying Qs from ops:
- For the registrant_id, can ON24 guarantee uniqueness across events (we sometimes have multi-event attendees)? We need a stable id per person, not per registration. If not, we'll need to dedupe on email in HubSpot.
- When the webhook fires, what headers should our endpoint expect for verification (HMAC, shared secret)?

Also: Laura, can you confirm whether we want the Zoom recording to be the canonical recording for Acme (they asked for a single hosted MP4), or should we use the ON24 archive as the source and stitch if needed?

On tests: Zoom support offered an additional stream test window on 5/21 at 10:00 PT — can we reserve that in case we need to test the RTMP switch?

—Tessa


From: Zoom Support <support@zoom.us>
To: Tessa Morgan <tessa_morgan@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 14 May 2026 14:10:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Joint ON24+Zoom webinar: registration mapping & stream fallback plan

Hello Tessa and Ava,

We can provide a dedicated RTMP ingest and a spare stream key for testing. For verification we recommend the following test slots: Thu 5/21 10:00 PT (reserved), Fri 5/22 09:00 PT (pre-rehearsal smoke), and Mon 5/25 08:00 PT (final). Please confirm which ones you'd like to hold.

Note on recording: Zoom cloud recording will produce a single MP4 and is exportable via API. If you want Zoom to be canonical, ensure the ON24 fallback triggers the RTMP to Zoom before ON24 disables recording.

Best,
Zoom Support Team
support@zoom.us


From: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa_morgan@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie_nguyen@redwood.com>, Priya Shah <priya@on24.com>

We're finalizing the configuration for the Acme Corp webinar on May 25 and I wanted to align on two things before we lock the rehearsal schedule: registration field mapping (ON24 -> HubSpot & Zoom) and the streaming fallback plan if the ON24 encoder hits bitrate limits.

We need: full name, email, company, job title, region, and a consent checkbox for marketing. Also, can ON24 pass a persistent registrant_id that we can use as a foreign key in HubSpot?

For streaming: our preference is to stream primarily through ON24 (primary) with Zoom webinar as an overflow/backfill stream. If ON24 encounters encoder bitrate spikes we want an automated fallback to a Zoom RTMP ingest (and record on Zoom). What capabilities does ON24 expose for automatic fallback, and do we need a separate RTMP endpoint from Zoom for this to work?

I've attached the ON24 production checklist and a proposed registration field list.

Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 12 May 2026 12:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Joint ON24+Zoom webinar: registration mapping & stream fallback plan

Hi Ava — thanks for the detailed note. Short answers below, followed by a recommended config.

1) Registration mapping: ON24 supports custom field API keys and will pass those back in the registration webhook payload. Use these keys: registrant_full_name, registrant_email, registrant_company, registrant_title, registrant_region, marketing_consent. We can also attach a persistent registrant_id in the webhook for matching to HubSpot. We'll send sample webhook payloads after this thread.

2) Fallback streaming: ON24 does not provide an automatic, seamless fallback to a third-party RTMP by default, but we can implement a heartbeat + script-based failover on our encoder layer. Practically that means provision a warm Zoom RTMP ingest endpoint and a small lambda (or ON24 event script) to trigger RTMP redirect when bitrate drops below threshold for X seconds. Yes, Zoom can accept RTMP and record on the Zoom side.

Recommended items (short):
- Provide Zoom RTMP endpoint and backup stream key.
- Agree on bitrate threshold (we suggest 2.5 Mbps for 720p slides + speaker audio).
- Confirm where recordings will live (ON24 archive vs Zoom cloud).
- Confirm webhook endpoint for registration POSTs and sample payload.

I'll attach a short checklist and sample webhook. Priya
ON24 CSM

title: Clarifications: EU AI Act deployment assurances & regression plan
preview:
From: Maria Gomez <maria.gomez@auroracapital.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-05-12T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Questions on EU AI Act annex for pilot deployment

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the quick demo last week. Our legal and compliance teams are pulling together the annex for high-risk systems and asked for a few clarifications around Redwood's deployment assurances and evaluation/regression approach. Could you confirm the following?

1) How do you map Redwood runtime controls to the EU AI Act obligations for high-risk systems (traceability, human oversight, accuracy monitoring)?
2) For model updates, what is your regression testing cadence and how do you surface performance drift to customers?
3) We need an example artifact showing traceability for a deployed model (inputs -> model version -> inference logs). A redacted sample would be fine.

If possible, can you send a short write-up and any artifacts by the end of next week? Procurement will need the assurance matrix for our SOW.

Best,
Maria
Maria Gomez
Head of AI Governance, Aurora Capital

Date: 2026-05-12T10:01:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Questions on EU AI Act annex for pilot deployment

Marissa — can you take point on assembling the matrix and the traceability sample? Neha and Jonas, tagging you for the technical mapping on NIST/EU obligations and the regression plan.

Thanks,
Ben
